steps.title: Compare tool treatment
steps.content: The crows keep hooked tools in their claws or put them in safe places when not using them. They do not protect basic sticks the same way. This difference suggests the hooked tools matter more to them.

steps.title: Reject unsupported behaviors
steps.content: Choice B focuses on ease of holding, but the text emphasizes protection and storage. Choice C invents sharing behavior. Choice D compares both tools with claws, but the passage never says claws are better or worse at removing prey from holes. The answer is A.
